title: S&T INC. Makeup Spatula, Large and Mini Silicone Spatula Set for Cosmetics, Shampoo, Hair Care, and Skin Care Products, 2 Piece Set, Black/Pink
description:
About this item
Set of 2 S&T INC. Makeup Tools - large spatula is 12 inches long with a 0.6 inch wide head and the mini spatula is 6 inches long with a 0.4 inch wide head
Flexible spatula is specially designed with silicone rubber spatula tips and plastic spatula handles, allowing you to use every drop of skincare products from bottles
Use the longer lotion spatula tool to get lotion out of bottle; great for body lotion, tanning lotion, sunscreen, body wash, or shampoo and conditioner set containers
Use the mini spatula silicone in small bottles or travel size toiletries like foundation makeup, serums, eye cream, sunscreen for face, moisturizer face cream, and other facial skin care products
Makeup bag and travel essentials; rinse the makeup bottle spatula with warm water and mild soap before and after each use, or place in the dishwasher for a deeper clean
reviews_5s:
Overall the spatulas are what I expected as far as materials and shape. From the photo, I kind of thought I was going to get a 6” & a 3” spatula. So I was very surprised when I got a 12” & a 6” spatula. It just didn’t look that big in the photo, and obviously I never read the dimensions so that’s my bad. It worked out even better though, because now I can use the larger spatula to scrape out big bottles of shampoo, conditioner, lotion, etc. Then I can use the smaller spatula to scrape out liquid foundation, lip gloss, tooth paste, face cream, travel-sized bottles, etc. I originally ordered this to use on make up items solely, I wasn’t even thinking about using it on larger bottles like big bottles of shampoo. So it ended up working out better than expected! I haven’t actually used the spatulas yet though. But, I use spatulas all the time for cooking and food preparation. I’m sure these will work exactly the same.

Update 3/13/21: In my original review I said I hadn’t use the spatulas yet. I flipping love these spatulas!! I seriously need more. I finally got around to scraping out all of my old foundation bottles like a week ago! I literally use the small one daily to mix things like homemade toothpaste for my dogs, mixing essential oils together for my skin or my dogs skin, mixing supplements with water so I can suck them up with a syringe for my sick dog. I literally use the small spatula every single day, multiple times a day. The big spatula I use for nearly empty shampoo, conditioner, or lotion bottles. I hate wasting stuff and this makes it easier for sure!!! Only thing I wish, is that there was a third spatula that had a flexible neck like a flexible plastic drinking straw. The reason that I say that is, because I can’t get the spatula to reach the top of any bottle, bevause of the angle. So if there was a way for me to change the angle of the spatula head, it would give me access to more of the bottles that I’m trying to empty out. Just a suggestion for the manufacturer!!! Anyway, I definitely recommend them. They’re super handy and multi-use tools! Especially if you’re in till like homeopathic remedies and stuff and you mix a lot of things at home yourself like I do. That’s my review, I hope it helps!If you're that woman that likes getting all of your foundation out of the bottom, it's a must have! I wash mine after each use.They do exactly what they're supposed to do. I wish I'd bought these sooner 😭I ordered these to remove my cover up. I was tired of either wasting 1/3 of the liquid left at the bottom or trying to find a way to get it out.

The stuff is expensive to waste so much of it. It comes with 2 paddles. 1 smaller thin long one which is the one I use for the makeup

The second one is pretty large. I put it in the kitchen and have used it to get condiments out of jars which has eliminated wasting so much and getting creams out.

Very well made rubber spatular for little spaces. You can't go wrong with these little guys. They are versitle, affordable and sturdy.

I would recommend them. Will save you money in the long runThose containers that have a lot of product left that the pump can't get have always been a pain, but not anymore! These little spatulas are great for getting those last bits out so that you don't throw any product in the trash. Highly recommend.I am so pleased with my purchase of these Beauty Spatulas (2 piece set), Large and Small Sizes for Beauty and Kitchen, Black/Pink! I bought them in order to allow me to scoop the last 3-4 ounces of conditioner out of a 1 L bottle of Paul Mitchell Tea Tree Lavender Mint Moisturizing Conditioner that I purchased. The pump on the 1 L bottle failed to get the last 3-4 ounces of conditioner out of the bottle … so I purchased these beauty spatulas & used the Large Beauty Spatula to scoop out the remaining 3-4 ounces that were left in the bottle. I am so pleased because those last 3-4 ounces are quite a bit of conditioner! If you have product left inside a bottle that you’re unable to get out … I highly recommend these Beauty Spatulas!I had two bottles of foundation, one had too little left to use the pump and the other was getting close. I had ways to work around it but I didn’t really have anything that worked great so I knew no matter what I wouldn’t be able to get all the foundation out.

These are a little bigger than I expected but the smaller of the two was perfect for scrapping up the sides of the first bottle and I was able to slowly transfer nearly all of the product to the second bottle.

I was getting close to buying more foundation but these spatulas saved me from doing that for a whileOver the years I've wasted to much product because I couldn't get to it!! These spatulas work just as advertised. Still can't get to the product that collects at the top of bottles, though...wish there was a bent or curved spatula for that....for now I just bend a Q tip to wipe it out.I bought this because I got tired of throwing out makeup/lotion bottles with product still at the bottom and sides. I am now on day 3 of scraping my primer and foundation bottles with the small spatula and looks like I still have a couple more days worth of product in the jar - YAY!...I just rinse the spatula after each use and it's ready to save me money again - definitely worth the money.....

10/28/19 - I just had to update - I originally thought I would get a few more days worth of product from the jars but it's been almost 2 month and I just got to the very last bit of these bottles...DEFINITELY WORTH BUYING!Personally, I feel like this is something that could be at the dollar tree. I don't think it is worth the price given that it it is literally just a plastic stick and a tiny tiny silicone scoop. With that said, it is a great product and works great for scooping out product and now I get much more days worth of product when before I would have had to throw those lotions, foundations, etc away. Maybe give it a few years and it will end up at the dollar tree or target dollar section for a more appropriate price.
reviews_1s: clearly has been used prior to sending to me. you can see grittiness and dirt. as well as the handle looks damagedI couldn't find my amazing spatula that bends and gets under the lip of the openings to get every last drop. These are NOT bendy at all and doesn't get all that serum/cream at top opening (some call it the shoulder area). Sure it gets the serum/creams at the bottom of the jar and the side but not the top. Meh. Will try to find something else. Tossing these.I bought these so I can use them when liquid glitters and concealers get low. As you can see in the picture, it doesn't fit! It is WAY too big! And I have no idea what I'm the world I am going to use the OBNOXIOUS big 1 for! On top of that, for something that is silicone, it is very difficult to clean. Don't waste your money. Buy a different one.I didn’t read the description which is my fault but I imagined these to be about hand size length. No. One is almost the length of my fore armRubber part fell off inside my makeup during the first use, making it unusable. I guess I could break my bottle of makeup to get the rubber part out and super glue it to the stick but that kind of defeats the whole purpose of this product. Don’t waste your money. Now I have to waste my time to mail it back to get a refund.These are RIDICULOUSLY long. Like, long enough to reach the bottom of a liter of shampoo but too wide to actually do so. They’re also too wide to function. Even the small one is too big to get into any product I own. These things are a joke. Total waste of money.Neither spatula fit easily into a standard make up or lotion/cream container. The small head spatula got stuck in the bottle and broke off on my first use. I had to throw away the spatula and the make up.I used the small spatula for getting makeup from the bottom of the bottle. It worked great but when I rubbed it with my fingertips under running water to cleaned it the spatula top came off and almost went down the drain. The larger sized spatula does not fit in a makeup bottle.I was using the small one in my concealer bottle to get the last little bit out. One swipe inside and the top broke off immediately. Not impressed. I have not used the large one, so I'm not sure if it's anymore sturdy or not.I NEVER write reviews! I was so excited to use this product so as not to waste my liquid foundation. This morning, upon FIRST USE, the tip of the spatula broke off! PIECE OF JUNK!!!
